sections.title: STANDARD FOR LONGANS
sections.text: Adopted in 1999. Amended in 2005, 2011.
sections.retrieval_text:
STANDARD FOR LONGANS
level=1
Adopted in 1999. Amended in 2005, 2011.

page_spans.text: Adopted in 1999. Amended in 2005, 2011.

sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of longans grown from Dimocarpus longan Lour., of the Sapindaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Longans for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial varieties of longans grown from Dimocarpus longan Lour., of the Sapindaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Longans for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of longans grown from Dimocarpus longan Lour., of the Sapindaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Longans for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY
number=2
level=1

sections.title: Minimum Requirements
sections.text: In all classes, subject to the special provisions for each class and the tolerances allowed, the longans must be: whole; sound, produce affected by rotting or deterioration such as to make it unfit for consumption is excluded; clean, practically free of any visible foreign matter; practically free of pests affecting the general appearance of the produce; practically free of damage caused by pests; free of abnormal external moisture, excluding top icing condensation following removal from cold storage; free of any foreign smell and/or taste1; fresh in appearance; free of damage caused by low and/or high temperatures; free of pronounced blemishes.

sections.title: Classification
sections.text: Longans are classified in three classes defined below:
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > Classification
number=2.2
level=2
Longans are classified in three classes defined below:

page_spans.text: Longans are classified in three classes defined below:

sections.title: The longans must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness in accordance with criteria proper to the variety and to the area in which they are grown.
path_titles: The longans must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness in accordance with criteria proper to the variety and to the area in which they are grown.
sections.text: The development and condition of the longans must be such as to enable them: to withstand transport and handling; and to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination. The colour of the longan’s flesh and skin may vary according to the variety. The longan’s skin may be lighter in colour than normal when treated by sulphur dioxide gas.

sections.title: Extra Class
sections.text: Longans in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Longans in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.text: Longans in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: slight skin defects such as bruising, scratches or other mechanical damage not exceeding a total area of 0.5 cm2.

page_spans.text: Longans in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: slight skin defects such as bruising, scratches or other mechanical damage not exceeding a total area of 0.5 cm2.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: This class includes longans which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the longans retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation: skin defects such as bruising, scratches or other mechanical damage not exceeding a total area of 0.5 cm2.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text: Size is determined by the number of fruits per kilogram or the diameter of the equatorial section of the fruit, in accordance with the following table: Size Code Number of Fruits (per Kilogram) Diameter (in millimetres) 1 < 85 > 28 2 85 	3 > 27 	28 3 95 	104 > 26 	27 4 105 	114 > 25 	26 5 > 115 24 	25

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.title: Size Tolerances
sections.text: For all classes, 20% by number or weight of longans corresponding to the size immediately above and/or below that indicated on the package for fruit sold in bunches and 10% for fruit sold individually.

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only longans of the same origin, variety , quality and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text: Longans must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new2, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Longans shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the longans. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.title: In Bunches
sections.text: In this case, each stem in a bunch should have at least three attached longans. The branch must not exceed 15 cm in length. A maximum of 10% by number or weight of detached fruit is allowed in each package.

classification.sentence: Longans for industrial processing are excluded.
classification.pattern: Subject + purpose qualifier + passive verb + exclusion
classification.justification: The sentence expresses a prohibition via exclusion using a passive construction with a subject qualified by purpose. This pattern does not strongly match any known templates, so it is classified as a new pattern within the Prohibition modality and exclusion communicative function.
notes: The sentence is a concise exclusion statement specifying a product and its intended use; this structure differs from typical active prohibition sentences.

sentences.original: In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:

notes: The sentence functions as a normative extension clause referencing another standard and adding obligations, which is distinct from typical obligation templates focused on direct action requirements.

sentences.original: The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

classification.sentence: The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).
classification.pattern: Subject + modal verb (should) + verb (comply) + object (criteria) + relative clause specifying criteria source

components.subject: The produce
components.criteria_source: Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997)

classification.justification: The sentence imposes an obligation by requiring compliance with established microbiological criteria. Its structure features a subject followed by a modal verb and verb phrase describing compliance with specific criteria referenced by a guideline. No exact or close template from the catalog matches this specific compliance citation structure, resulting in a new pattern classification.
notes: The modal 'should' here conveys an obligation in normative regulatory context.
The sentence references an external standard for criteria but does not specify numerical limits, hence falls under obligation rather than limits and targets.

sentences.original: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.

components.Obligation: each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce
components.Permission: each package may be labelled as to name of the variety

classification.justification: The sentence has a conditional antecedent followed by two coordinated modal expressions: obligation and permission. This combination with conditional modality and mixed normative statements does not strongly match known templates and requires a new structural pattern to capture the conditional plus dual modality coordination.
notes: The sentence blends condition, obligation, and permission within a single coordinated consequent, which is less common in typical regulatory templates and thus lacks close template match.

There is no strong or partial match to known templates, particularly as it's a list with optional notation, leading to classification as a new pattern under Limits and Targets modality with Permission modality and a specification communicative function.

The optional marking on 'Net weight' implies permission rather than obligation.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR LONGANS
pages.summary: Página inicial com o título principal do documento 'STANDARD FOR LONGANS' e código 'CXS 220-1999' como cabeçalho. Conteúdo breve indicando datas de adoção e emendas. Apenas um nó estrutural nível 1 correspondente ao título principal.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR LONGANS
pages.summary: A página apresenta o código e paginação no cabeçalho e título do documento na página anterior. Mapeia as seções principais 1 e 2 como nível 1; as subseções 2.1 e 2.2 como nível 2; e sub-subseções 2.1.1, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3 como nível 3. A seção 2 está marcada como continuando na próxima página devido à ausência de fechamento claro.

A página apresenta cabeçalho com código e paginação repetitivos. Mapeia as seções 3, 4 e 5 como nível 1 com seus respectivos níveis hierárquicos segundo a numeração. Textos associados aos títulos. A seção 5.2.1 é marcada como continua na próxima página para indicar que seu conteúdo segue.

A página apresenta hierarquia detectada do nível 1 ao nível 3, com seções principais: 5.3.1, 5.3.2, 6, 7 e 8 conforme numeração. Textos associados corretamente. Seções 5.3.1 e 5.3.2 são continuação da página anterior. Títulos de seções e subseções estão visíveis na página.
